title: Preserve Python wrapper shims for backwards compatibility during Go binary cutover
context: Porting the CLI codebase from Python to Go risked breaking external q-* skills that expect specific entry points.
content: To perform a big-bang cutover to the compiled Go CLI without breaking existing q-* skills, keep the main executable entrypoints (`agents` shell script wrapper) as Python-based shims that forward calls to the Go binary. This preserves backwards compatibility for the orchestrator toolchain while completing the underlying architecture migration.
anti_patterns: Completely removing Python wrappers before updating all external consumer skill references.
